analysis_description: Merged origin/main to obtain Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 (days 1–3) and round4work/. Re-read round4work/suggested direction.txt Phase 1, round4description.txt, outputs/r4_initial_analysis_summary.txt, ping_followup_phases.md (PHASE1 block). Implemented tape-only Phase 1 pipeline: r4_phase1_markouts.py joins trades to price mids; forward mid change is K **price rows ahead** per (day, product); horizons K in {5,20,100}; aggressor from trade price vs L1; spread tertiles; burst = >1 trade row at same (day,timestamp).
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate documented under round4_phase1_complete (nested). Phase 2 not started.

round4_phase1_complete.horizon_definition: For each trade at (day, timestamp, symbol), forward mid change at horizon K is mid(t+K rows) - mid(t) in the prices CSV for the same day and product, after sorting by timestamp (K consecutive snapshots, not wall-clock seconds).

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Mark 67 (mostly aggressive buyer) shows strongest same-symbol fwd_mid_20 (~1.85 ticks, n=165, frac_pos~0.65) stable days 1–3; Mark 49-only smaller. Mark 01 overall near-flat same-symbol at K=20 with low frac_pos; extract fwd when 01 appears modestly positive (~0.22).

phase1_bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (cell means + residual vs global mean fwd20)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Global mean same-symbol fwd_mid_20 ≈ 0.092. Top residuals: Mark 67→Mark 22 / Mark 67→Mark 49 on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT; Mark 55→Mark 14 on extract. Mark 01→Mark 22 on deep wings ~zero mean (negative residual vs global).
Dominant Mark 01→Mark 22; hydro Mark 14↔Mark 38. Two-hop motifs chain through Mark 22 as sink — consistent with initial sketch, no new mystery hub.
Burst timestamps: extract fwd K=5 lower than control (t≈-1.95); K=20 burst vs control not significant (t≈1.06). No strong short-horizon burst alpha on extract.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 5) Adverse selection proxy (aggressor vs same-symbol fwd20)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Aggressive buys mean fwd20 ~+0.31 (n=1500); aggressive sells ~-0.026 (n=2776) — buy-side aggression associates with better short-run mid markouts in this tape.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 67 aggressive buy touch: same-symbol fwd_mid_20 ~+1.8 ticks (n=164), stable days 1–3
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Low n; confirm per-symbol before sizing

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Aggressive buy vs sell same-symbol fwd20 gap (~0.34 tick difference in means)
Mark 14→Mark 38 on HYDROGEL_PACK positive residual fwd20 ~+0.23

negative_or_null_findings: Mark 01→Mark 22 basket on deep wings: ~zero or negative same-symbol fwd20 residual — no unconditional continuation on those prints.
Burst vs control extract fwd at K=20 not statistically convincing (Welch t≈1.06).
Mark 01 participant-wide mean fwd20 ~flat on same symbol despite huge volume — not a naive 'always follow 01' edge.
round4_phase1_complete.note: Phase 1 completion criteria met; Phase 2 documented in iteration 1.

analysis_description: Phase 2 per round4work/ping_followup_phases.md: ran r4_phase2_analysis.py on ROUND_4 days 1–3 — (1) Mark01→Mark22 basket burst timestamps vs matched control timestamps on extract forward; ±500ts near-burst flag for future use; (2) microprice vs mid and spread vs next-row |Δmid| on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT; (3) inner-join panel lead–lag corr(Δmid_extract, lag Δmid_VEV_5300); (4) Sonic joint gate (s5200≤2 & s5300≤2) splits for Mark67 and Mark55→Mark14 on extract; (5) wing skew proxy m4000−m6500 median split for Mark01→Mark22 fwd20; (6) passive-print bucket empty (n≈0 mid-touch trades); (7) per-day corr(cumulative signed qty per Mark, extract_fwd_20 at each row).
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate: round4_phase2_complete below. Phase 3 still blocked until ops send PHASE3 ping.

interaction_with_phase1: Mark 67 same-symbol fwd20: Phase 1 strong positive overall; Phase 2 Sonic split shows **no improvement** when joint gate tight (mean 2.06 n=27 vs 1.80 n=138 when wide) — small-n on tight; does **not** confirm Sonic as a cleaner for this Mark in R4 slice.
Mark 55→Mark 14 on extract (Phase 1 top residual): Sonic **tight** subset has **lower** mean fwd20 (0.44 vs 0.62 when gate wide) — gate **does not amplify** this Phase-1 edge here.

Basket bursts: Phase 1 mixed all burst-print rows vs controls; Phase 2 restricts to **one row per burst timestamp** on extract — K=20 mean extract fwd **0.263** (burst ts) vs **0.037** (control ts), n=314 each — **stronger** headline than Phase-1 all-row burst test (refines interpretation: timing aggregation matters).

Mark 01→Mark 22 unconditional wing continuation remains weak (Phase 1); Phase 2 adds **skew** split: high wing-skew proxy associates with **slightly negative** mean fwd20 vs low skew (+0.018) — suggests **fade / reduce size** on 01→22 ladder when m4000−m6500 is above median.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Synchronous panel: corr(Δmid_VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, Δmid_VEV_5300) ≈ **0.62** at lag 0 on inner-joined timestamps (days 1–3) — structural co-move for basket / hedge bots.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.orthogonal_to_phase1: Uses **price path** alignment, not counterparty-conditioned markout.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: **Post basket-burst timestamp** (Mark01→22, ≥3 symbols same ts): extract forward mean at K=20 rows ≈ **0.263** vs matched random timestamps **0.037** (n≈314) — event-study style continuation on extract after ladder prints.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.orthogonal_to_phase1: Refines burst statistic vs Phase-1 row-pooling.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Extract **microstructure**: corr(L1 spread, |Δmid next row|) ≈ **−0.22** — wider book tends to coincide with smaller next-step absolute mid move (vol proxy / mean-revert hint).

**Inventory proxy** (cumulative signed qty per Mark vs extract_fwd_20): Mark **22** negative corr each day (~−0.05 to −0.07); Mark **67** positive (~+0.05 to +0.06) — aligns Phase-1 story (67 buys vs 22 sells) with a path-level pressure metric.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.orthogonal_to_phase1: Aggregate path feature, not single-print markout.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Mark01→Mark22 prints when **wing skew proxy** (m4000−m6500) is **high** show slightly **negative** mean same-symbol fwd20 vs low-skew prints — conditional **fade** hypothesis for basket ladder in steep skew regimes.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.orthogonal_to_phase1: Adds smile/skew dimension Phase-1 tables did not stratify.

phase2_null_or_negative: Mid-passive prints (price strictly inside L1 at trade time) are negligible (n≈3) — Phase-2 passive adverse-selection table empty; tape mostly touch trades.
corr(microprice−mid, |Δmid next|) ≈ **0.01** — microprice imbalance does not predict next-step extract volatility here.
Sonic gate does **not** strengthen Mark55→Mark14 or Mark67 Phase-1 highlights on the splits we ran (often opposite sign / small n on tight).

analysis_description: Phase 3 per ping_followup_phases: r4_phase3_sonic_gate.py — R3-style inner join (5200,5300,extract) per day with dedupe per product per timestamp; tight=(s5200<=2)&(s5300<=2); extract K=20 forward on panel; spread–spread correlation matrix on pooled panel; trades merged to panel for sonic_tight at print time; three-way (buyer,seller,symbol,tight) mean fwd20 (min n>=15); Mark01→22 by symbol×gate; key cohorts by day×gate; inclineGod spread matrix s5200,s5300,s_ext.
used_to_decide: round4_phase3_complete documents Sonic replication on R4, spread panels, and whether gate cleans counterparty-conditioned signals vs Phase 1/2.

round4_phase3_complete.r3_convention_match: Same as analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py: inner join timestamps for VEV_5200, VEV_5300, VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT; first row per (product,timestamp); spread=ask1-bid1; tight=(s5200<=2)&(s5300<=2); fwd_k_extract=m_ext.shift(-K)-m_ext with K=20.

sonic_extract_forward_replication_r4.pooled_days_123: n_tight=7706 n_loose=22234; mean_fwd_extract_K20 tight=0.324 vs loose=-0.121; Welch t≈7.94 p≈2.3e-15; P(tight)≈0.257. Confirms Sonic-style mid edge on extract in R4 panel (mid-only).
sonic_extract_forward_replication_r4.by_day: p3_01_extract_fwdK_tight_vs_loose_by_day.csv — tight mean > loose each day; p-values strong days 1–3.
sonic_extract_forward_replication_r4.vs_phase1_2: Phase 2 showed trade-time Sonic splits did not help Mark55→14 / Mark67 aggregates; **panel-level** extract forward still strongly favors tight gate — distinction: **population timestamp** conditioning vs **sparse trade-time** subset.

inclineGod_spread_panels.spread_spread_matrix: p3_07_spread_spread_matrix_pooled.csv — corr(s5200,s5300)≈0.31; corr(s5200,s_ext)≈0.009; corr(s5300,s_ext)≈0.005 (extract L1 width nearly orthogonal to voucher gate legs in level correlation).
inclineGod_spread_panels.spread_vs_fwd_extract: p3_02: corr(s5200,fwd_k) and corr(s5300,fwd_k) weakly negative (~-0.05 to -0.07) per day — slight tendency for wider legs to associate with worse forward extract step (noise-dominated vs R3 summary magnitude).

counterparty_x_gate_synthesis.Mark01_Mark22: Pooled 01→22 all symbols: mean_fwd20≈0.0086; **n_loose=3** at merged trade timestamps (most basket prints occur when panel is tight) — gate does not separate 01→22 at trade time (Welch meaningless). Per-symbol table p3_04: deep wings still ~0 fwd; VEV_5200 shows positive mean only when tight (n=11).
counterparty_x_gate_synthesis.Mark67_Mark22_EXTRACT: **Tier-A three-way:** tight mean_fwd20≈2.83 (n=15) vs loose≈1.83 (n=60) — gate **amplifies** this niche pair on extract (p3_08 / p3_03). This **refines** Phase-1 Mark67 story: interaction exists for **67→22** on extract under joint tight books; not visible in Phase-2 coarse Mark67_any split.
counterparty_x_gate_synthesis.Mark55_Mark14_extract: Day-stable mixed: day1 tight>loose; day3 tight negative (p3_05) — not Tier-A; aligns Phase-2 finding that Sonic does not universally clean this pair.

round4_phase3_complete.does_gate_clean_signal_sonic_claim: For **extract mid forward** on the **aligned panel**, yes (large t-stats, all days). For **counterparty-conditioned** edges: **mixed** — global Mark splits often unchanged, but **Mark 67 → Mark 22 on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** shows higher mean fwd20 when tight (small n). Mark 01→22 basket remains structurally tied to tight timestamps so gate does not separate loose/tight at trades.

ranked_tier_edges_phase3.edge: (Mark 67, Mark 22, VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT) × sonic_tight: mean_fwd20 **2.83** (n=15 tight) vs **1.83** (n=60 loose)
ranked_tier_edges_phase3.caveat: Low n tight; validate with execution / hold horizon before sizing

ranked_tier_edges_phase3.edge: Panel extract K=20 forward: **tight** regimes outperform **loose** (pooled + per-day Welch)
ranked_tier_edges_phase3.caveat: Mid-only; bid/ask PnL may differ (STRATEGY caveat)

ranked_tier_edges_phase3.edge: corr(s5200,s5300)≈0.31 pooled — joint tight region is a real co-movement cluster (inclineGod)
Mark 14→Mark 38 on VEV_4000 when tight: mean_fwd20≈1.1 (n=55) — strong cell in p3_03
Mark 01→22 VEV_5200 prints when tight: mean_fwd20≈0.86 (n=11) — tiny n

round4_phase3_complete.phase4_note: trader_r4_v1.py implements Sonic gate sizing (not Mark-conditioned); see iteration 3.

analysis_description: Shipped trader_r4_v1.py: live Sonic joint gate (5200+5300 spread<=2) drives extract passive size and optional deep passive on wide VEV_4000 when gate tight. Documented backtest harness limitation: TradingState.market_trades is {} during Trader.run in prosperity4bt test_runner — counterparty-conditioned triggers from Phase 3 (e.g. Mark67→Mark22 on extract) cannot be evaluated in-sim without injecting tape trades before each decision or extending the runner.
used_to_decide: Gate-only policy is executable and profitable vs no-op on days 1–3 worse (12,203 vs 0); all-mode total 9,684 with weak day 2. **Update:** `TestRunner` now mirrors tape into `state.market_trades` before each run (see iteration 4); prior claim that field was always empty is superseded.

analysis_description: Patched imc-prosperity-4-backtester/prosperity4bt/test_runner.py: after building order_depths, copy `data.trades[timestamp][product]` into `state.market_trades[product]` so Round 4 strategies can read buyer/seller on tape prints. Shipped trader_r4_v2.py (v1 + Sonic_tight AND Mark67→Mark22 on extract → ex_buy min(12,7+2)). Backtest: PnL and merged tradeHistory MD5 **identical** to v1 — `min(ex_buy, buy_room)` almost never exceeds 7 on the sparse signal ticks (inventory long bias). Offline: 4 timestamps/day1 have both tight gate and 67→22 extract print.
used_to_decide: To realize Tier-A tape edge in sim, next step is **not** larger ex_buy when br<=7 — use **second order** (e.g. touch bid), **skew prices**, or **fade** path; or relax position / multi-level quotes. Harness now supports counterparty reads.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v3: Tier-A tick splits extract buy into touch-at-bb (up to 4) then bb+1 primary for remaining room + ex_buy bump. Ablated touch-only and reserve-after-primary; merged tradeHistory MD5 unchanged vs v1 on full days 1–3 (worse and all). Conclusion for this tape+matcher: **order-list reshuffle at same prices does not change realized fills** vs single Order in v1 — need different **prices**, **sell-side** reaction, or **non-passive** action to move PnL.
used_to_decide: Next sim experiments: signal-triggered **fair skew** (move bid/ask vs EMA), **reduce sell** on sig, or **take** small lift when tape prints at ask — each needs explicit worse-fill risk budget.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v4: Tier-A tick uses **different prices** than v1 (bb+2 bid, ask from ceil(fair)+2) plus asymmetric sizes (+1 buy cap 12, sell max(2,base-2)). Backtest: merged tradeHistory **differs** from v1 (4698 vs 4697 rows worse; +1 extract-side row). Worse PnL **12,185** vs v1 **12,203** (−18); day 3 extract line **5,289** vs **5,307**. All-mode total **9,666** vs **9,684**. Conclusion: skew binds on some signal ticks and **hurts** under worse matching here — next try smaller skew (bid+2 only, keep ask v1) or inventory-conditioned skew.
used_to_decide: Confirms analysis iteration 5: **price** change was required to move fills; v2/v3 order-split at same prices was a dead end.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v5 ablation: Tier-A **bid-only** skew (bb+2, buy+1 cap 12) while ask path and sell size stay **v1**. Worse: **12,203** and tradeHistory **identical** to v1 (same MD5, 2239 our fills) — bid+2 + buy bump still **does not bind** vs v1 under worse matching (same as v2 clip story). All-mode: **9,684** and MD5 **identical** to v1. Therefore v4's **−18** worse PnL vs v1 is attributed to **ask lift ceil(fair)+2** and/or **sell trim**, not the bid leg alone.
used_to_decide: If we want Tier-A to move worse PnL **positively**, isolate **ask-side** experiments carefully (smaller +1 step) or combine with inventory state; bid-only is a null on this tape.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v6: Tier-A **ask-only** skew (ceil(fair)+2 ask anchor on tight+Mark67→22 extract); bid/sizes match v1. Backtest worse **12,203** and tradeHistory MD5 **c775b003c67994dd** identical to v1 — ask path alone still does not bind vs v1 under worse matching. Logged in results.json iteration 8 with own_fill_rows (SUBMISSION rows) for auditable fill stats.
used_to_decide: Ask-only is a **null** for Tier-A execution on this tape (same as v5 bid-only story asymmetrically). Next: aggressive take (v8) or inventory-conditioned hybrid.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v7: Tier-A **sell-trim-only** (ex_sell=max(2,base-2) on tight+sig). Worse **12,185**, tradeHistory MD5 **f4c0352e485d22b8** matches v4 — confirms v4's **−18** slip is driven by **sell trim**, not bid+2 or ask lift in isolation.
used_to_decide: Avoid reducing passive sell clip on Tier-A ticks unless paired with a compensating bid-side or inventory rule.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v8: v1 + small **market-style buy** at best ask on Tier-A (Sonic tight AND Mark67→Mark22 on extract tape): qty 2 when buy_room>=6, position<=160, 25-tick cooldown; passive bid uses remaining room so limit check passes. Round 4 days 1–3 worse total **12,953** vs v1 **12,203** (+750); per-day +260/+326/+164. All-mode total **10,432** vs v1 **9,684** (+748). own_fill_rows 833 vs v1 818; extract own qty 3021 vs 2991 (+30). tradeHistory MD5 **247d64b0253dca4a** (differs from v1). Shows mid-forward Tier-A edge can translate to **PnL** when paying spread in a controlled way.
used_to_decide: Tune AGGR_BUY_Q / cooldown / min buy room vs inventory skew; optional **sell trim avoidance** on same ticks (v7 showed harm) — next iteration could gate aggression on position < threshold only.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v9: Tier-A aggression same as v8 but **AGGR_MAX_POSITION=100** vs 160. **Worse** days 1–3: total **12,953**, per-day and `tradeHistory` MD5 **247d64b0253dca4a** identical to v8 — the 100 cap **never binds** on the worse-fill path (signal ticks always have extract position ≤100 in this run). **All** mode: total **10,306** vs v8 **10,432** (−126); own_fill_rows 955 vs 958; MD5 **11c3b55a5aba8bde** — stricter cap perturbs tape-matching only under `all`, not a worse-mode improvement.
used_to_decide: Drop 100-cap branch for production unless we find ticks where pos>100 at signal; prefer **size** tuning (v10) or cooldown.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v10: v8 with **AGGR_BUY_Q=3** and **AGGR_MIN_BUY_ROOM=8** (cooldown 25, max pos 160). Round 4 days 1–3 **worse** total **13,327** vs v8 **12,953** (+374); per-day +130/+162/+82 vs v8. **All** total **10,807** vs v8 **10,432** (+375). own_fill_rows still **833** on worse (same fill-event count) but own extract qty **3036** vs **3021** (+15) — larger lift per qualifying signal. `tradeHistory` MD5 worse **4cb010168602bb29**. Day-stable improvement across all three days in worse mode.
used_to_decide: Ship v10 over v8 for this tape slice unless out-of-sample breaks; next sweeps: **cooldown 15 vs 40**, **AGGR_BUY_Q=4** with room≥10 (risk), or **skip aggression** when extract spread>2.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v11: v10 with **AGGR_COOLDOWN=15**. Backtest worse/all **identical** to v10 (13,327 / 10,807; `tradeHistory` MD5 **4cb010168602bb29** / **c58b6bd06c5f84f6**). Offline tape: Mark67→Mark22 on extract — **75** trade rows days 1–3; within-day consecutive gaps **min=700** ticks, **zero** gaps ≤25 or ≤40. **Conclusion:** cooldown 15/25/40 is **non-binding** on this tape; sweeps are null by construction.
used_to_decide: Next aggression sweeps should change **qty / min room** or **price level** (not cooldown) unless we find denser Tier-A clusters on other days.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v12: v10 with **AGGR_COOLDOWN=40**. Same null as v11 — **identical** PnL and `tradeHistory` to v10 (worse/all). Confirms long cooldown also irrelevant given sparse Tier-A timestamps.
used_to_decide: Keep v10 as representative; drop cooldown from tuning grid for R4 days 1–3 Tier-A.

analysis_description: trader_r4_v13: v10 + **extract L1 spread ≤2** gate before buy-at-ask. **Identical** to v10 on worse/all and `tradeHistory` — gate never excludes in sim (Tier-A ticks in this slice always have extract spread ≤2 when Sonic+tight+signal align), or redundant with existing filters. Evidence: same MD5 **4cb010168602bb29** / **c58b6bd06c5f84f6** as v10.
used_to_decide: Try **spread ≤1** or **microprice vs mid** gate next if we want microstructure filtering; or log `extract_spread` at signal ticks in a one-off script.
